channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Evolution of Gastrointestinal Inflammatory Diseases and Neoplasm Burden in Super-Elderly Populations: Integrated GBD 2023, CHARLS, and CLHLS Analyses of China and G20 Countries.
item.description: Molecular medicine (Cambridge, Mass.) - Rapid population aging has profoundly altered the epidemiological profile of gastrointestinal diseases in individuals aged ≥75 years. Although enteric infections (EI) have been effectively controll...

item.title: Integrative Transcriptomic Analysis Identifies Shared Immune-Fibrotic Transcriptional Programs Across Crohn's Disease and Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis.
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) and Crohn's disease (CD) share overlapping immune and fibrotic processes, yet their convergent molecular mechanisms remain poorly defined. Here, we performed an ...

item.title: Early-life gastrointestinal inflammation and the developing brain: Unravelling the pathways to long-term cognitive dysfunction.
item.description: World journal of clinical pediatrics - Review - The gut-brain axis (GBA) is a complex, bidirectional communication network critical to integrating central nervous system functions with gastrointestinal (GI) health. This review examines how disru...

item.title: Epigenetic reprogramming of tissue-resident memory T cells in chronic inflammatory disorders and implications for targeted therapies.
item.description: Epigenomics - Tissue-resident memory T (TRM) cells play a role in causing long-term tissue injury in chronic inflammatory diseases via pathological epigenetic reprogramming. Nevertheless, the epigenetic processe...

item.title: Diagnostic Accuracy of Salivary Periodontal Biomarkers Is Reduced in Patients With Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Clinical and experimental dental research - Patients with Crohn's disease (CD) are at increased risk of developing periodontal disease and may benefit from salivary biomarker-based monitoring for early detection and management. Given the pre...

item.title: Liver Injury From Medications Used for Treating Inflammatory Bowel Disease: The Drug-Induced Liver Injury Network Experience.
item.description: Liver international : official journal of the International Association for the Study of the Liver - We aimed to investigate and characterise DILI due to approved therapies for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Using the Drug-Induced Liver Injury Network (DILIN) prospective study, we evaluated def...

item.title: Beta-Glucan modulates monocyte plasticity and differentiation capacity to mitigate DSS-induced colitis.
item.description: eLife - Trained immunity involves the reprogramming of innate immune cells after an initial exposure, resulting in heightened inflammatory responses to subsequent stimuli and enhanced bactericidal capacity...

item.title: Beyond the Gut: How the Thymus Reshapes the Immune Landscape of Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Comprehensive Physiology - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) comprises a group of chronic inflammatory disorders of the gastrointestinal tract driven by immune dysregulation. In recent years, increasing attention has been dir...

item.title: Chishizhi Yuyuliang Decoction Ameliorates Ulcerative Colitis in Mice by Promoting Colonic Epithelial Mucosal Healing via the CAV1/FA Axis.
item.description: Rapid communications in mass spectrometry : RCM - This study aimed to investigate the therapeutic mechanisms of CY in treating UC using an integrated metabolomics and proteomics approach. A murine UC model was induced using dextran sulfate sodium ...

item.title: The Association of Faecal Calprotectin Measurements With Endo-Histological Remission in Ulcerative Colitis: A Pooled Analysis.
item.description: Alimentary pharmacology & therapeutics - Endoscopic and histologic evaluation is part of routine clinical practice to confirm the presence of active disease in ulcerative colitis. Faecal calprotectin (FCAL) levels show a positive correlat...

item.title: Association Between Toothbrushing Habits and Mucosal Healing in Japanese Patients with Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Evidence regarding the association between oral environment and the inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including ulcerative colitis (UC), exists. However, evidence regarding the association between ...

item.title: Concomitant psoriasis may be associated with a more severe disease course in inflammatory bowel disease: a retrospective cohort study.
item.description: Scientific reports - Psoriasis and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) share genetic and immunological features, but the impact of concomitant psoriasis on the clinical course of IBD is not well defined. We investigated w...

item.title: Anti-inflammatory effects of GLP1-RA drugs.
item.description: The Journal of clinical endocrinology and metabolism - Glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonists (GLP-1RA) are established treatment options for type 2 diabetes and obesity. This class of medications has redefined care by lowering glucose, reducing bod...

item.title: Chronic Liver Disease, Liver Damage and Liver Failure After Hypoabsorptive Bariatric Surgery: A Dose-Dependent Relationship and Multisystemic Consequences.
item.description: European journal of clinical investigation - Review - Metabolic bariatric surgery remains the most potent weapon we have against severe obesity and its metabolic consequences. Yet, its effects on the liver are far from uniform. Although restrictive an...

item.title: Surgical management of metastatic cutaneous Crohn's disease: a case series from a tertiary centre in the United Kingdom.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Metastatic Crohn's disease (MCD) is a rare extra-intestinal manifestation (EIM) of Crohn's disease (CD), defined by granulomatous inflammation of skin non-contiguous to the gastrointestinal tract. ...

item.title: Choosing NSAID treatment in axial spondyloarthritis: factors guiding clinical decision-making.
item.description: Current opinion in rheumatology - Review - Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) are first-line therapy for axial spondyloarthritis (axSpA), improving pain, function, and disease activity. This review evaluates evidence for NSAID ef...

item.title: Communicating Risk and Safety of Inflammatory Bowel Disease Therapies.
item.description: The American journal of gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) often require long-term treatment with advanced therapies, including biologics and small molecules. The use of these agents must be carefully balanced against the ...

item.title: Whole genome-based polygenic risk score and structural variation analysis in Korean patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: BMB reports - The specific roles of single-nucleotide polymorphisms and structural variants (SVs) in Korean patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) susceptibility remain unclear; hence this study aimed to...

item.title: Exploring causal associations between autoimmune diseases and hearing loss: a mendelian randomization study.
item.description: Brazilian journal of otorhinolaryngology - The causal relationship between Autoimmune Diseases (ADs) and Hearing Loss (HL) remains unclear. This study investigates whether genetic predispositions associated with ADs contribute to HL risk. M...

item.title: IL23R genetic variants associate with Crohn's disease risk and microbiome changes in healthy first-degree relatives.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - Single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) in the Interleukin-23 receptor (IL23-R) gene are associated with Crohn's disease (CD), suggesting a role in pathogenesis and several biologic agents targeting...
